disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A well-demarcated dark warty 'stuck-on'-looking lesion, one of several similar lesions increasing with age, stable until today's mechanical trauma
trigger_question_examples: Does it look 'stuck-on' and warty, and do you have several similar spots that increased with age?
Had it been stable/unchanged before it caught today?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, it looks stuck-on and warty, I have several similar spots that have increased with age, and it was stable until it caught on the towel today.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: It had NOT been changing/growing/darkening or bleeding spontaneously before today's snag (evolution screen)
trigger_question_examples: Before it caught today, had it been changing, growing, darkening or bleeding on its own?
Is this the odd one out compared to your other spots?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it hadn't been changing or bleeding on its own before — it only bled because I caught it with the towel; it looks like my other similar spots.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: On low-dose aspirin (antiplatelet), explaining easier bleeding
trigger_question_examples: Are you on aspirin or any blood thinners?
Do you take anything to thin your blood or for your heart?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, I take a low-dose aspirin daily.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Please just tell me it's not a melanoma that burst.
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Reassure appropriately but honestly: the described features favour a benign 'age spot' (seborrhoeic keratosis), and the bleeding is mechanical — but because a proper dermoscopic exam can't be done over video, recommend a routine in-person skin check to confirm (and biopsy anything atypical). Provide haemostasis advice. Should neither over-escalate to ED nor give absolute remote reassurance.
